page.title: Template engines
page.lead: To render views you must configure the templates folder with app.views(), register engines with app.engineTemplates(), and finally respond using Response.render().

setup.title: Base configuration
setup.description: The <mark class="docs-highlight">views()</mark> method defines where your views are located, and <mark class="docs-highlight">engineTemplates()</mark> maps an extension to its render engine.

render.title: Using Response.render()
render.description: After configuring folder and engine, use <mark class="docs-highlight">Response.render()</mark> to send the view with dynamic data.

compatible.title: Currently supported engines
compatible.description: Skyguard currently works with third-party engines like <mark class="docs-highlight">Express Handlebars</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">EJS</mark>, and <mark class="docs-highlight">Pug</mark>. In the future, a native template engine is planned.

handlebars.title: Express Handlebars
handlebars.description: Register the <mark class="docs-highlight">hbs</mark> extension and main layout configuration.

ejs.title: EJS
ejs.description: Map <mark class="docs-highlight">ejs</mark> using <mark class="docs-highlight">ejs.renderFile()</mark> so Skyguard can render views by extension.

pug.title: Pug
pug.description: Configure the <mark class="docs-highlight">pug</mark> extension with <mark class="docs-highlight">pug.renderFile()</mark> and use the same <mark class="docs-highlight">Response.render()</mark> API.
pug.tip: Keep a naming convention between file and view (for example <mark class="docs-highlight">dashboard.pug</mark> with <mark class="docs-highlight">Response.render("dashboard")</mark>) to avoid resolution errors.

warn: If you register multiple engines, the view extension defines which one is used for each render.
